rulings.Case Title: PAUL AKERS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 16, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. The Karolyn Myers declaration is hearsay as to its subject document. Ay's declaration in pertinent part relies on those documents. Ay's declaration contains much inadmissible testimony. (See below). 1. Defendant's objections Nos. 1,2,3, 4 & 5 to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. 2. Defendant's objections to Nos. 6, 7 (except for the first sentence) 8 (except for the first sentence,) 9, 10, 11, 12, and 13 to Ay's declaration are sustained. 3. Defendant's objections to Myers' declaration is sustained. (late tentative). =(302KOPP/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RUTH YAGER VS. AGCO CORPORATION,INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT PEREIRA VS. BENJAMIN PARK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 16, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT AIRPORT COMMUTER, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. 1. Plaintiff's request for continuance of the hearing in this 33 month old case is denied, the Court noting a prior two-month hearing continuance. 2. Both parties fail to comply with CCP section 437c and CRC Rule 3.1350. Defendant's notice of motion and separate statement are legally deficient. The three alternative theories of liability do not constitute "issues" or "defenses", much less a motion on "duty" and fail even collectively to dispose of plaintiff's entire case or separate causes of action. 3. Defendant's authority differ factually from the case at bar. Respondeat superior can apply on these facts. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. vs. Haight (1988) 205 Cal.App. 3rd 223, 240-244. 4. Defendant fails to show plaintiff cannot reasonably obtain evidence necessary to prove an element or claim or adduce evidence sufficient to shift its burden under the permissive use statute, negligent hiring or punitive damages. See Aguilar vs. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 845, 850-852). =(302KOPP/JW)

rulings.Case Title: 363 VALENCIA, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HILDELISA VASQUEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff 363 Valencia, Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 16, 2009, line 14, Plaintiff 363 Valencia Street LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment. PLAINTIFF 363 VALENCIA, LLC,'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The parties shall appear. Plaintiff furnishes conjectural argument about Dattari's claimed contradictory testimony regarding plaintiff's bona fide intent to abandon the rental of units and sell the building; but provides no supporting case law. =(302KOPP)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS SUCCESSOR VS. JONATHAN SHAPIRO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 16, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT JONATHAN SHAPIRO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. CCP section 92(d) allows a motion to strike only on the ground damages or relief sought are not supported by allegations. Plaintiff's prayer for restitution relates to its real property; its prayed-for damages relate to unlawful detainer damages only. Defendant's motion to strike is denied. Defendant shall file and serve an answer by April 21, 2009. =(302KOPP/xCH)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS SUCCESSOR VS. JONATHAN SHAPIRO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 16, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT JONATHAN SHAPIRO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant was legally served on June 23, 2008 as per CCP section 1162 (Ex. Nos. 2&3). CCP 1161b(a) took effect July 8, 2008. It does not apply retroactively. Plaintiff's thirty day Notice to Quit has been ignored; Defendant remains in possession of the premises: The Complaint alleges facts sufficent legally to state a cause of action and is not uncertain. Plaintiff alleges specifically current ownership of the property, Defendant's current tenancy and proper, service under CCP section 1162. The Demurrer is overruled. =(302KOPP/xCH)
